english: multifid leaves, like those of caraway Wán.; nyág-ga (nyag ga) méd-pa (med pa) not cleft, not indented. -- 4. of wool, nyág-tu (nyag tu) drén-pa ('dren pa) to draw out into threads, to spin Mil.
Cs., Sch. also nyag-nyóg (nyag nyog) filth, dirt.
Mil. = sna-tsogs (sna tshogs) (?), of rare occurrence.
thread; chain, of gold Mil., of iron Mil.; cord for stringing turkoises Mil.; a cable Schr.
scale of a steel-yard, nyag-rdó (nyag rdo) weight of a steel-yard.
a small beam, a pole Cs.; an arrow; nyag-p̊rán-mdá (nyag phran mda') arrow Mil.

also nyag-ré (nyag re), single; nyag rcig (nyag gcig) 1. id., skra (skra), or spu (spu) nyag(-ma) rcig (nyag ma gcig) a single hair, frq.; skrá-yi (skra yi) nyág-ma (nyag ma) id. (a man has 21 000 of them Med.) -- 2. a minimum Mil. -- 3. Sch. also: bachelor, old voluntary bachelor. -- saṅs-rgyas-nyag-rcíg (sangs rgyas nyag gcig) Thgy., Pth., only Buddha, or nothing less than Buddha.

(nyán-to (nyan to), nyán-tam (nyan tam)), imp. nyon (nyon) 1. (also, though seldom, mnyán-pa (mnyan pa)) c. dat. or accus. to hear, to give ear to, to listen (cf. tós-pa (thos pa)); slób-dpoṅ-gyi (slob dpon gyi) t́ád-du (thad du) ćos (chos) nyán-pa (nyan pa) to attend to the religious instruction of the teacher; ṅag (ngag) or tsig (tshig) nyán-pa (nyan pa) Dzl., ḱa-la (kha la), or resp. źál-la (zhal la), or bka-nyán-pa (bka' nyan pa) to obey, to yield; ṅas (ngas) j́i-ltar (ji ltar) zér-pai (zer ba'i) ḱa-la (kha la) nyán-na (nyan na) Glr., ṅa (nga) zer (zer) nyán-na (nyan na) Mil. if you listen to my word; Tar. 14, 14; 17, 16 c.c. las. -- 2. to listen secretly, to be an eaves-dropper, *ṕag-nyen (phag nyan) j́hê'-pa* (byed pa) C., *ṕag-nyán (phag nyan) ćò-će (byed byed), t́áṅ-će* (gtang byed) W., id.; nyán-mḱan (nyan mkhan) col. nyán(-pa)-po (nyan pa po), fem. nyan(-pa)-mo (nyan pa mo), B., a hearer, auditor; nyan-tós (nyan thos) id.; but esp of the personal disciples of Bud-dha, the Sravakas, Köpp. I., 419; Burn. I., 296; nyan-tós (nyan thos) bc̀u-drúg (bcu drug) the sixteen gnás-brtán (gnas brtan) q.v.; nyan-tós-ma (nyan thos ma) a female hearer; ḱa-la (kha la) nyán-po (nyan po), nyán-mḱan (nyan mkhan) obedient, ḱa-la (kha la) mi (mi) nyán-po (nyan po) disobedient. -- 3. to be able, later B., and col., gen. with a negative: gró (gro) ma (ma) nyán-pas (nyan pas) not being able to walk (on account of illness) Mil.; also like ma (ma) btúb-pa (btub pa) not being willing; without a negative: *nyán yin* (nyan yin) W. yes, I shall be able; inst. of rúṅ-ba (rung ba): *za-nyán yód-na kyoṅ* (za nyan yod na khyong) W., bring it me, if it is still eatable.
also nyam-tíg (nyam thig), nyam-yós (nyam yos) cricket, locust Sik.

resp. tugs (thugs), tugs-nyám(s) (thugs nyam(s)) 1. soul, mind, nyáms-kyi (nyams kyi) grogs (grogs) companions of the soul, viz. the murmuring springs and rivulets in the solitude of alpine regions Mil.; nyáms-kyi (nyams kyi) ćaṅ (chang) the soul's wine, i.e. religious knowledge Mil.; nyams dgá-ba (nyams dga' ba) 1. well being, comfort, cheerfulness, nyams mi-dgá-ba (nyams mi dga' ba) an unhappy state, discomfort, nyams-dgá (nyams dga') glú-ru (glu ru) bloṅs (blangs) sing a song of joy! Mil. 2. gen. adj.: agreeable, delightful, charming, nyáms-dga-bai (nyams dga' ba'i) sa-gnás (sa gnas) a charming country Glr.. -- 2. thought, nyams skye (nyams skye) or śar (shar) a thought rises. -- 3. strength, magnitude, height, state, manner, nyams-(kyi) (nyams kyi) tsád (tshad) byéd-pa (byed pa) Pth. (also with bćád-pa (bcad pa) or lén-pa (len pa) C.) to try, to put to the test, e.g. one's strength; tugs-dám-gyi (thugs dam gyi) nyams sád-pa (nyams sad pa) to try the degree of a person's devotion or spiritual progress Mil.; smra-nyáms (smra nyams), byed-nyáms (byed nyams) manner, -- and particularly a pleasing, agreeable manner, -- of speaking or dealing. Other phrases are: nyáms-su (nyams su) lén-pa (len pa) to take to heart, to interest one's self in or for a thing Dzl., to commit to memory, to learn (v. below); nyáms-su (nyams su) myóṅ-ba (myong ba) to suffer, undergo, experience Dzl.; nyams ṅá-ba (nyams nga ba) v. the compounds; nyams bćád-pa (nyams bcad pa) C. to try, to examine; nyams brú-ba (nyams bru ba) C. to irritate, provoke, vex; nyams myóṅ-ba (nyams myong ba) = nyáms-su (nyams su) myóṅ-ba (myong ba); nyams bźág-pa (nyams bzhag pa) is said to be = drán-pa (dran pa) nyé-bar (nye bar) bźág-pa (bzhag pa), v. nyé-ba (nye ba); nyams lén-pa (nyams len pa) 1. = nyáms-su (nyams su) lén-pa (len pa), v above, 2. col. to measure out, to
